title: BigP3BCI Study A — P300 BCI EEG dataset (13 healthy subjects, Mainsah et al. 2025)
description: BigP3BCI Study A is a derivative P300 brain-computer interface dataset containing 32-channel EEG recordings from 13 healthy subjects performing a visual speller task using a 6×6 checkerboard stimulus paradigm. The dataset comprises EEG data sampled at 256 Hz with binary classification of target and non-target events, annotated using HED 8.4.0 schema. This represents Study A of the largest public P300 BCI dataset and has been processed using the MOABB (Mother of All BCI Benchmarks) framework.
methods_description: EEG data were acquired using a g.USBamp amplifier (g.tec) with 32 channels in a standard 10-20 montage, sampled at 256 Hz with 60 Hz line frequency notch filtering. Subjects performed a visual P300 speller task using a 6×6 checkerboard stimulus paradigm with row-column and random presentation modes. Trial intervals were defined from 0 to 1.0 seconds post-stimulus.
